name: Orange-Currant Scones
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup (1 stick) chilled unsalted butter, cut into pieces
1/2 cup Chilled solid vegetable shortening, cut into pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 425°F.
2. Line a large baking sheet with foil.
3. In a large bowl, stir together the flour, 1/3 cup sugar, baking powder, salt, and baking soda until well blended.
4. Add the butter and shortening; using your fingertips, rub them into the mixture until it forms a coarse meal.
5. Stir in the buttermilk, currants, and orange peel until the dough comes together in large, moist clumps.
6. Gather the dough into a ball and turn it out onto a lightly floured work surface.
7. Gently knead the dough 3 or 4 times to combine well.
8. Divide the dough into 2 pieces and flatten each into rounds about 1/4-inch thick.
9. Using a floured knife, cut each round into wedges (about 8 per round).
10. Transfer the scones to the prepared baking sheet.
11. Brush the remaining buttermilk over the scones and sprinkle with additional sugar.
12. Bake until golden brown, about 15 minutes. Serve warm or at room temperature.
